questions.correct_answer: His lotus face, tender compassion, and playful sidelong glances
questions.explanation: The text lists Kṛṣṇa's lotus face, tender compassion, playful sidelong glances, and lovely mild smiling as qualities that are hard to find even among the demigods.

56.verse_text: On every path we see the childlike form of Murāri, whose one fixed vow is to relieve all the afflictions of the people whose hearts are blossoming with faith in Him. His soft, bright bluish cheeks glow with ever new effulgence.

questions.question: What specifically is identified as the singular, unwavering vow of the childlike Murāri?
options: To protect the sacred cows of Vṛndāvana
To relieve all the afflictions of those whose hearts blossom with faith in Him
To defeat the demons who disturb the peace of the innocent
To manifest His divine effulgence in the hearts of the sages
questions.correct_answer: To relieve all the afflictions of those whose hearts blossom with faith in Him
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that Murāri's one fixed vow is to relieve all the afflictions of the people whose hearts are blossoming with faith in Him.

questions.question: How is the physical appearance of Murāri's cheeks described?
options: As having the radiant glow of a rising golden sun
As soft and bright bluish, glowing with ever new effulgence
As marked by the touch of His beloved's cooling sandalwood paste
As delicate and pale like the petals of a blooming lotus
questions.correct_answer: As soft and bright bluish, glowing with ever new effulgence
questions.explanation: The text describes His cheeks as soft, bright bluish, and glowing with ever new effulgence.

57.verse_text: O girlfriend, who is this slowly coming along the path to Mathurā? His head is adorned with a peacock feather, and His body is more captivating than an emerald column. His eyes are rolling, and His face is beautified by the sweetness of His wonderfully fascinating smiles. His words, with their natural boyishness, are very soothing, and His natural grace would win praise from an elephant in rut.

questions.question: What standard of grace does the author use to describe the natural movement of the youthful Lord Kṛṣṇa as He travels toward Mathurā?
options: A soaring eagle
A dancing peacock
An elephant in rut
A playful lion cub
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the natural grace of the boy walking toward Mathurā would win praise from an elephant in rut.

questions.question: To what object is the captivating physical form of the boy traveling to Mathurā compared?
options: A mountain of gold
An emerald column
A lotus blossom
A moonlit cloud
questions.explanation: The verse describes the body of the boy coming along the path to Mathurā as being more captivating than an emerald column.

58.verse_text: Oh, what is this lustre in the form of a young boy! His feet, having in a contest totally conquered clusters of lotuses, are the shelter for Lakṣmī-devī. His hands, which express His love while playing the flute, are seats of artistic beauty. His arms, diffusing a flood of sweetness, are the vessel of desire's fulfilment for the doe-eyed milkmaids. And His face-ah, His face is beyond words.

questions.question: What specifically do the feet of the young boy Kṛṣṇa achieve in their contest?
options: They surpass the brilliance of the sun and moon
They totally conquer clusters of lotuses
They defeat the arrogance of the mountain peaks
They outshine the ornaments of the devas
questions.correct_answer: They totally conquer clusters of lotuses
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that the feet of the young boy have in a contest totally conquered clusters of lotuses.

questions.question: Which divine figure is identified as finding shelter at the feet of the young boy?
options: Sarasvatī-devī
Pārvatī-devī
Lakṣmī-devī
Rādhā-devī
questions.explanation: The text identifies the feet of the young boy as the shelter for Lakṣmī-devī.

questions.question: How do the arms of the young boy serve the doe-eyed milkmaids?
options: They act as a cooling shade from the sun
They serve as a vessel for the fulfilment of desire
They offer protection from the forest demons
They provide guidance through the dark forest
questions.correct_answer: They serve as a vessel for the fulfilment of desire
questions.explanation: The verse describes the arms as diffusing a flood of sweetness and serving as the vessel of desire's fulfilment for the doe-eyed milkmaids.

questions.question: What is the artistic significance of the hands of the young boy mentioned in the verse?
options: They hold the divine conch and discus
They are seats of artistic beauty while playing the flute
They paint the scenery of Vṛndāvana
They distribute the dust of the forest
questions.correct_answer: They are seats of artistic beauty while playing the flute
questions.explanation: The verse states that His hands, which express His love while playing the flute, are seats of artistic beauty.

59.verse_text: Indeed, His face, abundantly adorned with lovely lips that possesses two or three specially arranged waves of lustre, needs no other ornament. The wealth of His artistic attributes and amorous gestures are beyond the understanding of fools. Oh, oh, oh, how wonderful, how amazing, how very, very wonderfully amazing is this splendour [in the form of the young boy Kṛṣṇa]!

questions.question: What unique feature adorns the lips of the young boy Kṛṣṇa, rendering any other ornament unnecessary?
options: A string of glistening pearls
Two or three specially arranged waves of lustre
A soft, radiant golden glow
Delicate markings resembling lotus petals
questions.correct_answer: Two or three specially arranged waves of lustre
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that His face is adorned with lovely lips that possess two or three specially arranged waves of lustre, which makes any other ornament unnecessary.

questions.question: Why does the author state that the wealth of Kṛṣṇa's artistic attributes and amorous gestures remain hidden?
options: Because they are reserved for the residents of Vaikuntha
Because they are beyond the understanding of fools
Because they are too subtle for human language to describe
Because the gopis keep them a secret
questions.correct_answer: Because they are beyond the understanding of fools
questions.explanation: The text directly mentions that the wealth of His artistic attributes and amorous gestures are beyond the understanding of fools.

60.verse_text: Oh, what an ineffable sportive beauty Kṛṣṇa has fully revealed before me! In all directions my eye witnesses this beauty. But alas, alas, He is beyond my grasp! O mother, the three worlds are filled with my longed-for youth, Kṛṣṇa!

questions.question: Where does the speaker report witnessing the sportive beauty of Kṛṣṇa?
options: Within the deepest meditation of the heart
In all directions
Upon the banks of the Yamunā
In the reflection of the clear waters
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that in all directions the eye witnesses Kṛṣṇa's beauty.

questions.question: What limitation does the speaker lament regarding their experience of Kṛṣṇa's beauty?
options: That the beauty is only visible at night
That the beauty cannot be fully described by words
That Kṛṣṇa is beyond their grasp
That the beauty is hidden from the three worlds
questions.correct_answer: That Kṛṣṇa is beyond their grasp
questions.explanation: The speaker explicitly laments with the words 'alas, alas, He is beyond my grasp!' after witnessing His beauty.

questions.question: To whom does the speaker appeal when declaring that the three worlds are filled with Kṛṣṇa's youth?
options: The gopīs
The mother
The sages
The Supreme Lord
questions.explanation: The speaker directly addresses 'O mother' while stating that the three worlds are filled with their longed-for youth, Kṛṣṇa.

61.verse_text: Oh, when [shall I again fasten into a top-know] my Lord's thick locks, [lying] like separate rows of bees [on His shoulders]? When [shall I hear] His mild words, [see] His large eyes, [kiss] His sweet lips, [see] His sweetly beautiful face, and [experience] His fickle nature?

questions.question: To what feature of the Lord's appearance does the author compare His thick locks of hair resting upon His shoulders?
options: Dark monsoon clouds
Separate rows of bees
A cluster of black lotus flowers
Strings of dark silk
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly describes the Lord's thick locks as lying like separate rows of bees on His shoulders.

questions.question: Beyond fastening the Lord's hair and gazing at His large eyes, what specific interaction does the author yearn for regarding the Lord's lips?
options: To offer them a cooling drink
To kiss them
To place a flute upon them
To whisper secrets into them
questions.explanation: The verse lists the specific actions the author longs to perform, including kissing the Lord's sweet lips.

questions.question: Which of the Lord's characteristics does the author long to personally experience in their meditation?
options: His fierce warrior spirit
His fickle nature
His profound silence
His unwavering stillness
questions.explanation: The verse concludes with the author expressing a desire to experience the fickle nature of the Lord.

62.verse_text: If the Lord, the friend of the distressed, is surrounded by the beguiling wound of His flute, when will He be able to hear us repeatedly crying out, "O abode of mercy, please protect us!"

questions.question: What specifically is described as a 'beguiling wound' that surrounds the Lord who is the friend of the distressed?
options: The nectar of His smile
The melody of His flute
The scent of His garland
The glance of His lotus eyes
questions.explanation: The verse identifies the beguiling sound of the Lord's flute as a wound that surrounds Him.

questions.question: How do the devotees address the Lord in their plea for protection while He is surrounded by the enchanting sound of His flute?
options: O protector of the universe
O master of the cows
O abode of mercy
O friend of the fallen
questions.explanation: The devotees explicitly call out to the Lord as the 'abode of mercy' while seeking His protection.

63.verse_text: Kṛṣṇa, an ocean of mercy, bears the fragrance of fresh youth. When oh when will some danger to me focus His attention so that He looks upon me with His wide eyes?

questions.question: What quality does the poet attribute to the fragrance of Kṛṣṇa in this verse?
options: The scent of celestial flowers
The fragrance of fresh youth
The aroma of sandalwood paste
The sweet smell of the forest
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that Kṛṣṇa bears the fragrance of fresh youth.

questions.question: What is the specific physical feature that the speaker longs for Kṛṣṇa to cast upon them when they are in danger?
options: His cooling smile
His lotus-like feet
His wide eyes
His compassionate hands
questions.explanation: The speaker asks when some danger will cause Kṛṣṇa to look upon them with His wide eyes.

questions.question: What metaphorical description is given for Kṛṣṇa's capacity for mercy in this verse?
options: A deep sapphire
An ocean of mercy
A blossoming flower
A brilliant sun
questions.explanation: The verse directly refers to Kṛṣṇa as an ocean of mercy.

64.verse_text: Oh, that I may see that young boy, who has a complexion as deep blue as a sapphire; sweet lips like bimba fruit; charming, mild smiling; soothing, nectarous words; cooling glances; and large, dawn-red eyes, and who is famous for His flute-song.

questions.question: What specific comparison is used to describe the color of Kṛṣṇa's complexion?
options: A dark monsoon cloud
A deep blue sapphire
The depth of the Yamunā river
A peacock feather
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly describes the young boy as having a complexion as deep blue as a sapphire.

questions.question: Which fruit is invoked to describe the sweetness of Kṛṣṇa’s lips?
options: Mango fruit
Bimba fruit
Pomegranate fruit
Berry fruit
questions.explanation: The verse states that Kṛṣṇa possesses sweet lips like bimba fruit.

questions.question: What is the unique color attributed to Kṛṣṇa's large eyes?
options: Lotus-petal pink
Deep ocean blue
Dawn-red
Golden-yellow
questions.explanation: The verse describes Kṛṣṇa as having large, dawn-red eyes.

questions.question: For what specific musical activity is Kṛṣṇa famous in this verse?
options: His divine dancing
His flute-song
His beautiful singing
His enchanting laughter
questions.explanation: The verse concludes by stating that the young boy is famous for His flute-song.

65.verse_text: The ineffable adolescence of Kṛṣṇa, the father of Cupid, is sweeter than sweetness yet fickler than fickleness. Alas, that adolescence has stolen away my heart! What shall I do now?

questions.question: What two contradictory qualities define the ineffable adolescence of Kṛṣṇa, the father of Cupid?
options: Sweeter than nectar and colder than ice
Sweeter than sweetness and fickler than fickleness
More radiant than the sun and deeper than the ocean
More playful than a deer and sharper than a thunderbolt
questions.correct_answer: Sweeter than sweetness and fickler than fickleness
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that the adolescence of Kṛṣṇa, the father of Cupid, is sweeter than sweetness and fickler than fickleness.

questions.question: Whom does the author identify as the father of Cupid within the context of describing the Lord's adolescence?
options: Lord Brahmā
Lord Śiva
Lord Kṛṣṇa
Lord Nārāyaṇa
questions.explanation: The text refers to the adolescence of Kṛṣṇa, the father of Cupid, stealing away the author's heart.

66.verse_text: Oh, when shall I see that young boy (Kṛṣṇa), who is an ocean of jubilation and a treasury of playfulness? Broad are His lotus eyes and His chest, mild are His gentle smiles and His loving talks, sweet are His cherry-red lips and His flute-song.

questions.question: Which specific attributes of the young Kṛṣṇa are described as being 'sweet' in His manifestation as an ocean of jubilation?
options: His lotus eyes and chest
His cherry-red lips and flute-song
His gentle smiles and loving talks
His peacock feather and glancing gaze
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that sweet are His cherry-red lips and His flute-song.

questions.question: What physical features are described as 'broad' in the description of the young boy Kṛṣṇa?
options: His cherry-red lips and lotus eyes
His lotus eyes and chest
His chest and flute-song
His gentle smiles and loving talks
questions.explanation: The verse describes that broad are His lotus eyes and His chest.

questions.question: In the meditation upon the young boy Kṛṣṇa, what two characteristics are noted as being 'mild'?
options: His flute-song and loving talks
His lotus eyes and cherry-red lips
His gentle smiles and loving talks
His ocean of jubilation and treasury of playfulness
questions.correct_answer: His gentle smiles and loving talks
questions.explanation: The verse states that mild are His gentle smiles and His loving talks.

67.verse_text: The fortunate, who have heaped up pious merit, can see (Kṛṣṇa) the original person, who brings their eyes under control with His extremely tender glances, whose lips are sweet with the nectar of his open smile, and who has adorned His head with a peacock feather.

questions.question: What specifically brings the eyes of the fortunate ones under control according to the descriptions of Lord Kṛṣṇa?
options: His flute music
His extremely tender glances
The peacock feather on His head
His cherry-red lips
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that the original person (Kṛṣṇa) brings the eyes of those who have heaped up pious merit under control with His extremely tender glances.

questions.question: With what item is Lord Kṛṣṇa described as having adorned His head in this verse?
options: A garland of forest flowers
A crown of gold
A peacock feather
A cluster of kadamba blossoms
questions.explanation: The verse describes Kṛṣṇa as the one who has adorned His head with a peacock feather.

questions.question: What is the source of sweetness for the lips of the original person mentioned in the verse?
options: The nectar of His open smile
The sound of His flute
The touch of the gopīs
The sweetness of forest honey
questions.correct_answer: The nectar of His open smile
questions.explanation: The verse states that Kṛṣṇa's lips are sweet with the nectar of His open smile.

68.verse_text: My dear friends, where is that Kṛṣṇa, Cupid personified, who has the effulgence of a kadamba flower, who is sweetness itself, the nectar for my eyes and mind, He who loosens the hair of the gopīs, who is the supreme source of transcendental bliss, and my life and soul? Has He come before my eyes again?

questions.question: To what object is the bodily effulgence of Lord Kṛṣṇa compared as He is addressed as Cupid personified?
options: A budding lotus flower
A kadamba flower
A golden champaka flower
A blossoming jasmine flower
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly describes Kṛṣṇa as having the effulgence of a kadamba flower.

questions.question: Which specific action performed by Kṛṣṇa is mentioned in the prayer as He is described as the source of transcendental bliss?
options: He plays the melody of the flute
He steals the garments of the gopīs
He loosens the hair of the gopīs
He dances in the forest of Vṛndāvana
questions.correct_answer: He loosens the hair of the gopīs
questions.explanation: The text identifies Kṛṣṇa as the one who loosens the hair of the gopīs.

69.verse_text: This boy (Kṛṣṇa), with His dancing eyes, His face beautifying all directions, and His charming outfit just suitable for a cowherd, has extracted the milk of joy for our eyes.

questions.question: What effect does the sight of Kṛṣṇa, in His charming cowherd outfit and dancing eyes, have upon the observers?
options: He steals the butter from their homes
He extracts the milk of joy for their eyes
He captivates their hearts with the sound of His flute
He destroys the pride of the demons
questions.correct_answer: He extracts the milk of joy for their eyes
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that this boy, with His dancing eyes and cowherd outfit, has extracted the milk of joy for our eyes.

questions.question: Which specific features of Kṛṣṇa contribute to His face beautifying all directions?
options: His peacock feather and tinkling ornaments
His dancing eyes and charming outfit suitable for a cowherd
His lotus face and gentle, tender smile
His swinging forearms and restless, love-agitated gaze
questions.correct_answer: His dancing eyes and charming outfit suitable for a cowherd
questions.explanation: The verse identifies Kṛṣṇa's dancing eyes and His charming cowherd outfit as the elements that make His face beautify all directions.

70.verse_text: He comes before us-the cooling balm for our eyes-wearing many tinkling ornaments on His body and a peacock feather on His head. His forearms are swinging, His eyes move restlessly with the agitation of love, and the moon of His lotus face displays a tender, gentle smile.

questions.question: What specifically causes the restless movement of Lord Kṛṣṇa's eyes as He appears?
options: The playful sight of the gopīs
The agitation of love
The sound of His tinkling ornaments
The bright reflection of the moon
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that His eyes move restlessly with the agitation of love.

questions.question: Which adornment is specifically mentioned as being worn upon the head of the Lord as He appears?
options: A golden crown
A garland of forest flowers
A peacock feather
A band of pearls
questions.explanation: The verse describes the Lord appearing with a peacock feather on His head.

questions.question: To what does the author compare the moon when describing the features of the Lord's face?
options: His gentle smile
His lotus face
His cooling eyes
His swinging forearms
questions.explanation: The verse refers to the moon of His lotus face displaying a tender, gentle smile.

71.verse_text: This child (Kṛṣṇa), who is the ornament for the intimate community of cowherd boys and girls, whose lively glances are so soothing, and whose moon-face has a wealth of soft, tender smiles, gladdens my heart and enters deep within it.

questions.question: What specifically describes the effect Kṛṣṇa has on the heart of the speaker as He enters within it?
options: He awakens deep spiritual longing
He bestows the wealth of liberation
He gladdens the heart
He dispels all material ignorance
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that this child Kṛṣṇa gladdens my heart and enters deep within it.

questions.question: How is Kṛṣṇa characterized in relation to the community of cowherd boys and girls?
options: As the protector of their village
As the ornament of their intimate community
As the leader of their daily pastimes
As the supreme judge of their actions
questions.correct_answer: As the ornament of their intimate community
questions.explanation: The verse describes Kṛṣṇa as the ornament for the intimate community of cowherd boys and girls.

questions.question: What feature of Kṛṣṇa's moon-face is described as a wealth in this verse?
options: His restless, darting eyes
His soft, tender smiles
His cooling, soothing glances
His radiant, divine luster
questions.explanation: The text states that Kṛṣṇa's moon-face has a wealth of soft, tender smiles.

72.verse_text: How wonderful! The sound of the flute Kṛṣṇa is holding to His lips is pouring forth an indescribable flood of divine love before our very eyes! This is rare even among the planets of the demigods. Kṛṣṇa, our darling, our God, our very life, delights the three worlds.

questions.question: What is described as an indescribable flood of divine love that flows before our very eyes?
options: The nectar flowing from the flute of Kṛṣṇa
The radiance emanating from the moon-face of Kṛṣṇa
The sound produced by the flute Kṛṣṇa holds to His lips
The lively and soothing glances of the darling child
questions.correct_answer: The sound produced by the flute Kṛṣṇa holds to His lips
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that the sound of the flute Kṛṣṇa is holding to His lips is pouring forth an indescribable flood of divine love.

questions.question: Regarding the phenomenon poured forth by Kṛṣṇa's flute, what is noted about its availability or frequency?
options: It is common throughout the terrestrial world
It is rare even among the planets of the demigods
It is only available to those who dwell in the spiritual realm
It is experienced once every age by the inhabitants of the three worlds
questions.correct_answer: It is rare even among the planets of the demigods
questions.explanation: The text states that the flood of divine love produced by the flute is rare even among the planets of the demigods.

73.verse_text: This person approaching me is dark bluish like a tamāla tree. His charming eyes, which resemble stars, move restlessly, the shining orb of His moon-face is overflowing with delight [or, His face radiates joy like the rising moon], and He sportively begins sounding His flute. He is my very life.

questions.question: What comparison is used to describe the complexion of the person approaching in the verse?
options: A dark monsoon cloud
A tamāla tree
A deep blue lotus
The night sky
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly describes the person as being dark bluish like a tamāla tree.

questions.question: To which celestial object are the charming, restless eyes of the Lord compared?
options: The sun
The moon
The stars
The planets
questions.explanation: The text states that the Lord's charming eyes resemble stars.

questions.question: Besides sounding His flute, what distinct visual quality is attributed to the moon-face of the Lord?
options: It is overflowing with delight
It is adorned with a peacock feather
It is cooling the heat of Vraja
It is shaded by a lotus leaf
questions.explanation: The text describes the shining orb of His moon-face as overflowing with delight.

74.verse_text: Here is that Kṛṣṇa, my very life! He is the acme of fickleness, the one ultimate end of the existence of the fickle-minded milkmaids, the epitome of cleverness, the extreme perfection of the artistic skill of four-faced Brahmā, the limit of good fragrance, the utmost embodiment of all sorts of astounding pastimes, the culmination of good fortune, and the zenith of auspiciousness for Vraja.

questions.question: Whose artistic skill is described as having reached its extreme perfection in the form of Lord Kṛṣṇa?
options: Lord Śiva
The four-faced Brahmā
The sage Bilvamaṅgala
Indra, the king of heaven
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly describes Kṛṣṇa as the extreme perfection of the artistic skill of four-faced Brahmā.

questions.question: What is Kṛṣṇa identified as being the 'ultimate end of the existence' for?
options: The cowherd boys of Vraja
The fickle-minded milkmaids
The sages residing in the forest
The residents of Dvārakā
questions.explanation: The text states that Kṛṣṇa is the one ultimate end of the existence of the fickle-minded milkmaids.

questions.question: In the context of Vraja, what is Kṛṣṇa described as being the zenith of?
options: Auspiciousness
Intelligence
Strength
Beauty
questions.explanation: The verse characterizes Kṛṣṇa as the zenith of auspiciousness for Vraja.

75.verse_text: Oh! My good deeds have reached their culmination: Kṛṣṇa has appeared before my eyes! The moon of His face is made doubly refreshing by its sweet tenderness, and He sprinkles me with the current of nectar dropping note by note from the holes of His flute. He is the object of my words, which, though mad, are fortunate [to be describing Him].

questions.question: What effect does the flute's music have on the speaker as it flows from the holes of Kṛṣṇa's flute?
options: It washes away all sins of the past
It sprinkles the speaker with a current of nectar
It compels the speaker to dance in ecstasy
It calls the speaker toward the forest of Vraja
questions.correct_answer: It sprinkles the speaker with a current of nectar
questions.explanation: The verse states that Kṛṣṇa sprinkles the speaker with the current of nectar dropping note by note from the holes of His flute.

questions.question: How is the moon-like face of Kṛṣṇa described in its appearance to the observer?
options: It is glowing with the brilliance of a thousand suns
It is made doubly refreshing by its sweet tenderness
It is cooling the burning heat of the material world
It is adorned with the luster of a fresh lotus
questions.correct_answer: It is made doubly refreshing by its sweet tenderness
questions.explanation: The text explicitly describes the moon of His face as being made doubly refreshing by its sweet tenderness.

questions.question: How does the speaker characterize their own words in their attempt to describe Kṛṣṇa?
options: As being empty and devoid of true devotion
As being mad, yet fortunate to describe Him
As being a collection of scriptures and prayers
As being insufficient to capture His infinite glory
questions.correct_answer: As being mad, yet fortunate to describe Him
questions.explanation: The verse notes that Kṛṣṇa is the object of the speaker's words, which are described as being mad but fortunate to be describing Him.

76.verse_text: I bow down to the personified splendour (Kṛṣṇa), who is the protector of the cows-and also all the worlds-and who reclines on the sloping breasts of Rādhā-and also on Śeṣanāga.

questions.question: What dual-meaning is attributed to the reclining posture of Kṛṣṇa upon the sloping breasts of Rādhā?
options: His role as both the protector of cows and the sustainer of the universes
His identity as both the beloved of the gopīs and the master of the devas
His dual nature as the supreme person and the maintainer of the Vedas
His position as both the inhabitant of Vṛndāvana and the foundation of Śeṣanāga
questions.correct_answer: His role as both the protector of cows and the sustainer of the universes
questions.explanation: The verse identifies Kṛṣṇa as the protector of the cows and also all the worlds, while describing Him reclining on the sloping breasts of Rādhā and also on Śeṣanāga.

77.verse_text: I bow again and again before Him (Kṛṣṇa), whose glowing complexion is embellished with the kuṅkuma powder glorified by its place on the breasts of the cowherd men's wives, who is the original creator of the flute song, and who possesses the glory of innumerable Brahmās.

questions.question: What specifically embellishes the glowing complexion of Kṛṣṇa in his act of devotion?
options: Sandalwood paste from the forests of Vṛndāvana
Kuṅkuma powder from the breasts of the cowherd men's wives
The dust of the path traveled by the surabhi cows
Golden ornaments gifted by the queens of Dvārakā
questions.correct_answer: Kuṅkuma powder from the breasts of the cowherd men's wives
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that Kṛṣṇa's glowing complexion is embellished with the kuṅkuma powder glorified by its place on the breasts of the cowherd men's wives.

questions.question: With what superlative is Kṛṣṇa described regarding his flute song?
options: The eternal inspirer of the flute song
The original creator of the flute song
The master of the flute song's melody
The singular performer of the flute song
questions.correct_answer: The original creator of the flute song
questions.explanation: The verse identifies Kṛṣṇa as the original creator of the flute song.

questions.question: The glory of which entity is compared to that possessed by Kṛṣṇa?
options: The demigods of the heavenly planets
The great sages of the forest
Innumerable Brahmās
The primary manifestations of Lord Viṣṇu
questions.explanation: The verse states that Kṛṣṇa possesses the glory of innumerable Brahmās.

78.verse_text: Here comes my very life [Kṛṣṇa]! Having assumed a sporting attitude, He slowly approaches on tender lotus feet weighed down by softly tinkling anklets as He recollects various charming flute-songs.

questions.question: What specifically causes the tinkling sound emanating from Lord Kṛṣṇa's feet as He approaches?
options: The gentle clinking of golden toe rings
The movement of His softly tinkling anklets
The friction of His lotus feet against the earth
The weight of His heavy silken garments
questions.correct_answer: The movement of His softly tinkling anklets
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly mentions that Kṛṣṇa approaches on tender lotus feet that are weighed down by softly tinkling anklets.

questions.question: What is Lord Kṛṣṇa occupied with as He slowly approaches?
options: Searching for the lost cows in the forest
Recollecting various charming flute-songs
Calling out to the gopīs by name
Arranging flowers for His peacock crown
questions.explanation: The text states that as Kṛṣṇa approaches, He is recollecting various charming flute-songs.

questions.question: What is the physical demeanor Lord Kṛṣṇa assumes while approaching in this verse?
options: A stoic and meditative posture
A fierce and protective warrior stance
A sporting attitude
A hurried and restless pace
questions.explanation: The verse describes that Kṛṣṇa has assumed a sporting attitude as He approaches.

79.verse_text: Here comes [my very life, Kṛṣṇa]! The beauty of His playful sidelong glances overflows with bliss, and He sprinkles my two perked-up ears with the nectar of His sportive flute-songs. He is the only friend for my eyes.

questions.question: What specifically does Kṛṣṇa sprinkle into the author's perked-up ears?
options: The nectar of His sportive flute-songs
The divine words of the Vedas
The cooling water of the Yamunā
The pollen from a forest lotus
questions.correct_answer: The nectar of His sportive flute-songs
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Kṛṣṇa sprinkles the author's two perked-up ears with the nectar of His sportive flute-songs.

questions.question: How does the author characterize the beauty of Kṛṣṇa's sidelong glances?
options: As overflowing with bliss
As sharp like arrows
As heavy with longing
As reflecting the twilight sun
questions.explanation: The verse describes the beauty of Kṛṣṇa's playful sidelong glances as overflowing with bliss.

questions.question: What unique role does Kṛṣṇa hold in relation to the author's eyes?
options: He is the only friend
He is the only refuge
He is the only light
He is the only ornament
questions.explanation: The text identifies Kṛṣṇa as the only friend for the author's eyes.

80.verse_text: Seeing me from a distance with a flood of sidelong glances, my Lord walks toward me with the sportive grace of an elephant and moves my heart with the current of flute-sound emanating from His mouth, which is filled with brightly glowing teeth.

questions.question: What comparison does the poet use to describe the graceful manner in which the Lord approaches him?
options: The steady pace of a swan
The sportive grace of an elephant
The swift movement of a deer
The rhythmic stride of a lion
questions.correct_answer: The sportive grace of an elephant
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that the Lord walks toward the poet with the sportive grace of an elephant.

questions.question: Besides the sound of the flute, what specific physical feature of the Lord's mouth is highlighted while He is playing?
options: His reddish lips
His brightly glowing teeth
His gentle, smiling cheeks
His glistening tongue
questions.explanation: The verse mentions that the flute-sound emanates from the Lord's mouth, which is described as being filled with brightly glowing teeth.

81.verse_text: This is He! This is He-my Lord [Kṛṣṇa], playing on His flute! He possesses two wondrous feet, which are the shelter for the shelterless, which glow with the brilliance of many ornaments, which move this way and that, which are abundant with divine pastimes, and which fill the three worlds with the mellows of love.

questions.question: What primary characteristic defines the two feet of Lord Kṛṣṇa in terms of their effect on the three worlds?
options: They destroy all material desires
They fill the three worlds with the mellows of love
They manifest the form of the cosmic creation
They offer protection against all demonic forces
questions.correct_answer: They fill the three worlds with the mellows of love
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Kṛṣṇa's two wondrous feet fill the three worlds with the mellows of love.

questions.question: How are the feet of Lord Kṛṣṇa described in relation to those who are without any refuge?
options: They are the ultimate destination for the liberated
They are the source of all spiritual knowledge
They are the shelter for the shelterless
They are the foundation of the Vṛndāvana forest
questions.correct_answer: They are the shelter for the shelterless
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly describes the feet of Kṛṣṇa as the shelter for the shelterless.

questions.question: What is the source of the brilliance seen on Lord Kṛṣṇa’s feet?
options: The nectar flowing from His flute
The light emanating from His divine skin
The brilliance of many ornaments
The reflection of the three worlds
questions.explanation: The text states that Kṛṣṇa’s feet glow with the brilliance of many ornaments.

82.verse_text: This is He, who removed the burning distresses of great sages. This is He, who stole the clothes of the impassioned wives of the cowherd men of Vraja. This is He, who curbed the pride of Indra, lord of the third world (the heavenly realm). This is He, who has stolen the lotus of my heart.

questions.question: Which specific action performed by Kṛṣṇa demonstrates His curbing of the pride of the lord of the third world?
options: Lifting the mountain in Vraja
Stealing the clothes of the gopīs
Removing the burning distresses of the sages
Stealing the lotus of the heart
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly mentions Kṛṣṇa curbed the pride of Indra, the lord of the third world (the heavenly realm), through His pastime.

questions.question: Whose burning distresses did Kṛṣṇa remove according to the description of His activities?
options: The residents of Dvārakā
The wives of the cowherd men
Great sages
The demigods of the heavenly realm
questions.explanation: The text states that He is the one who removed the burning distresses of great sages.

questions.question: What object is metaphorically described as having been stolen by Kṛṣṇa from the author?
options: The flute of the cowherd boy
The clothes of the cowherd wives
The lotus of the heart
The pride of the sages
questions.explanation: The verse concludes by identifying Kṛṣṇa as the one who has stolen the lotus of the author's heart.

83.verse_text: This personified glory [Kṛṣṇa] has attained all excellence, both in His omniscience and His innocence. Having entered my eye, He is enjoying great bliss. This is a great wonder!

questions.question: What two qualities define the excellence achieved by the personified glory of Kṛṣṇa?
options: His omniscience and His innocence
His beauty and His strength
His playful nature and His wisdom
His transcendental abode and His eternal form
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that this personified glory has attained all excellence in both His omniscience and His innocence.

questions.question: Where is Kṛṣṇa residing while experiencing great bliss?
options: Within the author's heart
Within the author's eye
Within the realm of Vraja
Within the ocean of consciousness
questions.explanation: The text states that Kṛṣṇa has entered the author's eye and is enjoying great bliss there.

84.verse_text: With the rising of His moon-like face this certain person known as Kṛṣṇa is nourishing the faded lustre of the cooling moon and doubling the ocean of my thirst [to see Him]. He is my very life.

questions.question: What effect does the appearance of Kṛṣṇa’s moon-like face have on the luster of the cooling moon?
options: It extinguishes the moon's light entirely
It nourishes the moon's faded luster
It eclipses the moon's brightness
It reflects the moon's light upon the ocean
questions.correct_answer: It nourishes the moon's faded luster
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that the rising of Kṛṣṇa's moon-like face nourishes the faded lustre of the cooling moon.

questions.question: How does the author describe the impact of Kṛṣṇa’s presence on his own internal state of thirst?
options: It completely satiates his longing
It doubles the ocean of his thirst
It makes his thirst disappear
It causes his thirst to transform into indifference
questions.correct_answer: It doubles the ocean of his thirst
questions.explanation: The text states that Kṛṣṇa is doubling the ocean of the author's thirst to see Him.

85.verse_text: Again and again I mentally kiss Murāri's lotus face, with its sweet lips and its beautiful, slightly reddish eyes, which grace all His humble devotees.

questions.question: Which specific facial features of Murāri are described as being kissed repeatedly in the mind of the devotee?
options: His lotus face with sweet lips and slightly reddish eyes
His moon-like face with a smiling mouth and dark lotus eyes
His beautiful forehead with golden lips and lotus eyes
His lotus-like eyes with slightly red lips and a charming chin
questions.correct_answer: His lotus face with sweet lips and slightly reddish eyes
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states the devotee mentally kisses Murāri's lotus face, noting the presence of sweet lips and slightly reddish eyes.

questions.question: What is the effect of Murāri's lotus face on His humble devotees?
options: It grants them liberation from the cycle of birth and death
It bestows grace upon them
It provides them with the nectar of immortality
It fulfills all their material desires
questions.explanation: The verse indicates that Murāri's lotus face, with its sweet lips and slightly reddish eyes, graces all His humble devotees.

86.verse_text: Behold this nectar for the eyes, the lustre of this young boy Kṛṣṇa! His hands teach graceful gestures to the autumnal lotus, His feet excel freshly grown desire-tree sprouts in tenderness, and the beauty of His eyes destroys the pride of all comparable things.

questions.question: Which two parts of Kṛṣṇa's transcendental form are specifically compared to the autumnal lotus and the sprouts of a desire-tree?
options: His eyes and His hands
His hands and His feet
His face and His chest
His lips and His palms
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that Kṛṣṇa's hands teach graceful gestures to the autumnal lotus, while His feet excel the tenderness of freshly grown desire-tree sprouts.

questions.question: What is the effect of the beauty of Kṛṣṇa's eyes according to the description of His youthful form?
options: It inspires the autumnal lotus to bloom
It provides nectar for the eyes of the devotees
It destroys the pride of all things that might be compared to it
It outshines the tenderness of the desire-tree sprouts
questions.correct_answer: It destroys the pride of all things that might be compared to it
questions.explanation: The text states that the beauty of Kṛṣṇa's eyes destroys the pride of all comparable things.

87.verse_text: The bliss manifesting itself [in the wondrous lustre of the young boy Kṛṣṇa] reigns supreme between the sloping breasts of the beautiful milkmaids of Vraja. That spreading, glowing bliss reaches a rarefied stage far beyond the appreciation of the eyes of anyone by the milkmaids of Vṛndāvana, and every day, every moment, and every twinkling of an eye that bliss, embodied as Kṛṣṇa, arranges pastimes and attracts even Arundhatī [chastity personified] with the gentle charm of His tender smile.

questions.question: Which personality, representing the personification of chastity, finds herself attracted to the gentle charm of Kṛṣṇa's smile?
options: Rādhārāṇī
Arundhatī
Yaśodā
Devakī
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the bliss embodied as Kṛṣṇa arranges pastimes and attracts even Arundhatī, who is identified as chastity personified, with the gentle charm of His tender smile.

questions.question: Where does the supreme bliss manifesting in the lustre of the young boy Kṛṣṇa reign?
options: Between the sloping breasts of the milkmaids of Vraja
In the heart of the forest of Vṛndāvana
Upon the banks of the Yamunā river
Within the lotus of the hearts of the sages
questions.correct_answer: Between the sloping breasts of the milkmaids of Vraja
questions.explanation: The text states that the bliss manifesting itself in the wondrous lustre of the young boy Kṛṣṇa reigns supreme between the sloping breasts of the beautiful milkmaids of Vraja.

88.verse_text: All glories to Kṛṣṇa, my very life and the enchanter of the three worlds! His now-blooming youth is adorned with the last flickering of childhood. His eyes flashing with delight, He bewilders Cupid with the nectar of His smile. His beauty beguiles at every moment and out of love He drinks from the mouth of His flute.

questions.question: Whom does Kṛṣṇa bewilder with the nectar of His smile?
options: The residents of Vraja
Cupid
The milkmaids of Vṛndāvana
Arundhatī
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that Kṛṣṇa, with eyes flashing with delight, bewilders Cupid with the nectar of His smile.

questions.question: What activity does Kṛṣṇa perform out of love as described in the verse?
options: He dances with the gopīs
He drinks from the mouth of His flute
He gazes at the moon
He wanders in the forests of Vṛndāvana
questions.correct_answer: He drinks from the mouth of His flute
questions.explanation: The verse states that out of love, Kṛṣṇa drinks from the mouth of His flute.

questions.question: How does the author characterize the stage of Kṛṣṇa's physical development?
options: As an infant protected by mother Yaśodā
As a fully mature sovereign ruler
As a now-blooming youth adorned with the last flickering of childhood
As a transcendent form beyond all stages of age
questions.correct_answer: As a now-blooming youth adorned with the last flickering of childhood
questions.explanation: The verse describes Kṛṣṇa's age specifically as his now-blooming youth being adorned with the last flickering of childhood.

89.verse_text: How wondrous are these lotus feet of that [Lord Kṛṣṇa]! How wondrous these lotus eyes! How wondrous this lotus face! Oh, how wondrous, wondrous is this body!

questions.question: Which specific body parts of Lord Kṛṣṇa are described by the author as being wondrous in their lotus-like nature?
options: The feet, eyes, and face
The hands, feet, and eyes
The face, eyes, and chest
The feet, chest, and face
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly identifies the lotus feet, lotus eyes, and lotus face of Lord Kṛṣṇa as wondrous.

90.verse_text: I bow down to [Kṛṣṇa, who is] the only ornament of the entire world, yet who is ornamented by the pitcher-like breasts of Rādhā. He is the great, central jewel among the necklace of emerald-like young girls of Vraja.

questions.question: What title is bestowed upon Lord Kṛṣṇa in his capacity as the central jewel within the necklace of the young girls of Vraja?
options: The sole decoration of the entire universe
The crest jewel of the gopīs of Vṛndāvana
The emerald-like ornament of the Vraja damsels
The great, central jewel among the necklace of emerald-like young girls of Vraja
questions.correct_answer: The great, central jewel among the necklace of emerald-like young girls of Vraja
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly describes Kṛṣṇa as the great, central jewel among the necklace of emerald-like young girls of Vraja.

questions.question: How is Lord Kṛṣṇa described in relation to the pitcher-like breasts of Rādhā?
options: He is the one who kisses them in secret
He is the one who is ornamented by them
He is the one who hides behind them during pastimes
He is the one who finds them to be the source of all world beauty
questions.correct_answer: He is the one who is ornamented by them
questions.explanation: The verse identifies Kṛṣṇa as being ornamented by the pitcher-like breasts of Rādhā.

91.verse_text: Oh, what ineffable beauty my Lord Kṛṣṇa is displaying-a delightful beauty enhanced by a loveliness acquired when the fresh kuṅkuma on His darling's [Rādhā's] breasts scattered over His own body as He grabbed them [and She fought Him off], and enhanced as well by the drops of perspiration that seem to play on the mirror-like orbs of His cheeks.

questions.question: What specifically causes the appearance of the drops of perspiration that play upon the mirror-like orbs of Lord Kṛṣṇa's cheeks?
options: The intensity of the Vṛndāvana summer sun
The exertion from playful resistance offered by Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī
The deep ecstatic absorption in the melody of His flute
The physical struggle encountered while dancing in the rāsa-līlā
questions.correct_answer: The exertion from playful resistance offered by Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī
questions.explanation: The verse describes the beauty enhanced by perspiration on His cheeks while He is in the act of grabbing Rādhā, who is fighting Him off.

questions.question: Which substance contributes to the enhanced loveliness of Lord Kṛṣṇa's body during His affectionate encounter with His darling?
options: Sandalwood paste applied by the gopīs
Drops of nectar falling from the moon
Fresh kuṅkuma scattered from the breasts of Rādhā
Fragrant forest flowers gathered in Vraja
questions.correct_answer: Fresh kuṅkuma scattered from the breasts of Rādhā
questions.explanation: The text states that the beauty of Lord Kṛṣṇa is enhanced by the loveliness acquired when the fresh kuṅkuma on His darling's breasts scattered over His own body.

92.verse_text: O my Lord, the transcendental body of Kṛṣṇa is very sweet, and His face is even sweeter than His body. The soft smile on His face, which is like the fragrance of honey, is sweeter still.

questions.question: What specifically is compared to the fragrance of honey in the description of Lord Kṛṣṇa's transcendental features?
options: The soft smile on His face
The perspiration on His cheeks
The scent of the kuṅkuma on His body
The sound of His voice
questions.correct_answer: The soft smile on His face
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that the soft smile on Kṛṣṇa's face is like the fragrance of honey.

questions.question: In the hierarchy of the sweetness of Lord Kṛṣṇa's features, which aspect is considered the most sweet?
options: His entire transcendental body
His eyes
His face
His soft smile
questions.explanation: The verse establishes a progression, noting the body is sweet, the face is sweeter than the body, and the soft smile is sweeter still.

93.verse_text: I take shelter of [Kṛṣṇa], who is the shelter of everyone in the universe, who has the form of a human being, who is adorned with a peacock feather, and who is the be-all and end-all of the conjugal mellow.

questions.question: What specifically serves as the ornamental decoration upon the head of the human-like form of the Lord who is the shelter of all?
options: A garland of forest flowers
A crown of gold and jewels
A singular peacock feather
A string of fragrant lotuses
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly describes the Lord, who takes a human-like form, as being adorned with a peacock feather.

questions.question: Regarding the conjugal mellow, what is the position of the Lord whom the author seeks as his shelter?
options: He is the initiator of the conjugal mellow
He is the be-all and end-all of the conjugal mellow
He is the supreme enjoyer of the conjugal mellow
He is the hidden source of the conjugal mellow
questions.correct_answer: He is the be-all and end-all of the conjugal mellow
questions.explanation: The text identifies the Lord, who is the shelter of everyone, as the be-all and end-all of the conjugal mellow.

94.verse_text: O Lord, even to this very day thousands of Upaniṣadic sages have not seen you with their eyes or in their hearts. So by what great mercy of Your's have You come within the range of my distressed vision for so long?

questions.question: What specifically causes the author to question how Kṛṣṇa has come within his vision, given that even the Upaniṣadic sages have not seen Him?
options: The infinite power of the Lord's universal form
The great mercy of the Lord
The sage's long-term penance and austerity
The profound wisdom gained from the Upaniṣads
questions.correct_answer: The great mercy of the Lord
questions.explanation: The text explicitly asks by what great mercy of Kṛṣṇa He has come within the range of the author's vision, contrasting this with the thousands of Upaniṣadic sages who have not seen Him.

questions.question: How does the author describe his own state when contemplating Kṛṣṇa's appearance before him?
options: Exalted and enlightened by Vedic wisdom
Humble and feeling unworthy of the Lord's glance
Distressed
Overjoyed and liberated from all suffering
questions.explanation: The verse specifically refers to the author's vision as 'my distressed vision,' highlighting the personal state of the speaker receiving the Lord's presence.

95.verse_text: O Keśava, what is this bright glow of Your moon-face? What is this appearance of Your's, which is beyond description? Let that [glow] and that [appearance] be relished [by Yourself or by those souls competent to do so]. I simply bow down before You again and again, and yet again.

questions.question: When addressing Keśava regarding the splendor of His moon-face and the indescribable nature of His form, what act does the devotee repeatedly perform?
options: Singing hymns of glorification
Offering obeisances again and again
Meditation on the divine feet
Dancing in transcendental ecstasy
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that the speaker simply bows down before Keśava again and again, and yet again, in response to His indescribable beauty.

questions.question: How does the author characterize the nature of Keśava’s appearance in this prayer?
options: As a manifestation of the eternal Vaikuṇṭha form
As being beyond description
As the ultimate object of meditative focus
As a vision granted only to the sages
questions.explanation: The verse specifically inquires about the appearance of the Lord, describing it as an appearance which is beyond description.

96.verse_text: O Lord, the moon, having been conquered by Your moonlike face, has taken shelter at Your feet and split into ten parts-that is, Your toenails-thereby gaining much greater brilliance and beauty than before. O Lord, how great is even a slight manifestation of Your mercy!

questions.question: What entity is described as having taken shelter at the feet of Lord Kṛṣṇa after being conquered by His moonlike face?
options: The Sun
The Moon
The Lotus flower
The starry night sky
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that the moon, having been conquered by the Lord's moonlike face, has taken shelter at His feet.

questions.question: Into how many parts is the moon said to have split while taking shelter at the Lord's lotus feet?
options: Two parts
Five parts
Ten parts
Twelve parts
questions.explanation: The verse mentions that the moon split into ten parts, which are identified as the Lord's toenails.

questions.question: What physical feature of the Lord is compared to the ten parts of the moon that split after seeking refuge at His feet?
options: His fingertips
His toenails
His ankle bells
His lotus petals
questions.explanation: The verse describes the moon splitting into ten parts, which are characterized as the Lord's toenails, thereby gaining increased beauty.

97.verse_text: O Kṛṣṇa, how can anyone compare Your face to the lotus? And as for the moon- it attains a simply unmentionable state on the new-moon day. So what else can I say? What other thing in the universe could possibly equal the loveliness of Your face?

questions.question: Why does the author dismiss the moon as a valid comparison for the beauty of Lord Kṛṣṇa's face?
options: It lacks the brilliance of the lotus flower.
It reaches an unmentionable state on the new-moon day.
It is constantly hidden by the clouds of Vṛndāvana.
It is too far away to observe closely.
questions.correct_answer: It reaches an unmentionable state on the new-moon day.
questions.explanation: The verse states that as for the moon, it attains a simply unmentionable state on the new-moon day, making it an unsuitable comparison for the loveliness of Kṛṣṇa's face.

questions.question: Regarding the standard metaphors for Lord Kṛṣṇa's face, what does the author conclude about the lotus flower?
options: It is the only object in the universe worthy of comparison.
It is inferior because it only blooms during the day.
It cannot be compared to His face at all.
It is a beautiful reflection of His divine features.
questions.correct_answer: It cannot be compared to His face at all.
questions.explanation: The text explicitly asks how anyone can compare the face of Kṛṣṇa to the lotus, implying such a comparison is impossible.

98.verse_text: If you want to hear, then listen carefully to something that even the incomparable poets of old did not take note of. And that is this: The lamp of the moon deserves to bear for a long time the burden of worshiping your moon-face in the ārati ceremony.

questions.question: What unique service does the author suggest the moon perform for Kṛṣṇa’s moon-face?
options: To serve as a cooling shade during the midday heat
To perform the ārati ceremony by bearing the burden of worship
To reflect the brilliance of His smile back onto the world
To dissolve itself into the ocean of nectar found in His presence
questions.correct_answer: To perform the ārati ceremony by bearing the burden of worship
questions.explanation: The verse states that the moon deserves to bear the burden of worshiping Kṛṣṇa’s moon-face in the ārati ceremony.

99.verse_text: All glories to Your cooling smiles, which are an overflowing ocean of nectar destroying all other joys with unbroken streams of the elixir of bliss!

questions.question: What effect does the cooling smile of Lord Kṛṣṇa have upon other types of happiness?
options: It elevates them to a higher spiritual platform
It destroys them by providing an ocean of nectar
It serves as a temporary distraction from them
It acts as a gateway to understanding transcendental joy
questions.correct_answer: It destroys them by providing an ocean of nectar
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly describes the cooling smiles of the Lord as an overflowing ocean of nectar that destroys all other joys.

questions.question: In what specific manner does the elixir of bliss flow from the cooling smile of the Lord?
options: Through intermittent flashes of mercy
As a subtle scent of transcendental fragrance
In unbroken streams
Through the rhythmic sound of a flute
questions.explanation: The text characterizes the smile as an overflowing ocean of nectar, delivering the elixir of bliss through unbroken streams.

100.verse_text: Let there be thousands of men who possess relishable beauty, and let there be thousands of men who are steady in the glory of the fragrance of attractiveness. We shall neither quarrel with them nor speak well of them. But the truth, O Lord, is that in You the perfection of attractive beauty has reached its limit.

questions.question: What is the author's stated disposition toward others who possess relishable beauty or the fragrance of attractiveness?
options: He challenges them to a debate regarding the nature of aesthetics
He expresses deep envy for their transient worldly charm
He remains indifferent, choosing neither to quarrel with nor praise them
He argues that their beauty is a delusion meant to distract the devotee
questions.correct_answer: He remains indifferent, choosing neither to quarrel with nor praise them
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states, 'We shall neither quarrel with them nor speak well of them,' regarding those who possess relishable beauty or the fragrance of attractiveness.

questions.question: Regarding the nature of beauty, what conclusion does the author reach concerning the Lord?
options: The Lord's beauty is the standard by which all others are measured
The perfection of attractive beauty has reached its limit in the Lord
The Lord is the source of all beauty found within the material world
The Lord's beauty is hidden from the eyes of those who pursue worldly charms
questions.correct_answer: The perfection of attractive beauty has reached its limit in the Lord
questions.explanation: The verse concludes by declaring to the Lord, 'the truth, O Lord, is that in You the perfection of attractive beauty has reached its limit.'

101.verse_text: O Kṛṣṇa, being situated in You, the following things have achieved the fruit of their transient existences: the wives of the cowherd men who become restless, their shyness destroyed, and who turn humble with love for You; songs, which become swollen with passion; Your excess of frivolity, which becomes very sweet; and my fully elated words, which scatter sweetness by stringing a garland [of verses about You].

questions.question: What happens to the shyness of the wives of the cowherd men when they become situated in Kṛṣṇa?
options: It is transformed into divine radiance
It is completely destroyed
It is protected by His grace
It is hidden by their love
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that for the wives of the cowherd men who are situated in Kṛṣṇa, their shyness is destroyed.

questions.question: What is the specific result of the author's fully elated words regarding the Lord?
options: They achieve the status of sacred scriptures
They bring peace to the three worlds
They scatter sweetness by stringing a garland of verses
They silence the pride of the demigods
questions.correct_answer: They scatter sweetness by stringing a garland of verses
questions.explanation: The verse describes the author's fully elated words as scattering sweetness by stringing a garland of verses about Kṛṣṇa.

questions.question: What becomes 'very sweet' when situated in Kṛṣṇa according to the description of His attributes?
options: The flute music
His excess of frivolity
The glances of the gopīs
The beauty of Vṛndāvana
questions.explanation: The text identifies Kṛṣṇa's excess of frivolity as the element that becomes very sweet when situated in Him.

102.verse_text: O Lord, although the entire universe is Your abode, Lakṣmī is Your consort, Lord Brahmā and Pradyumna and Your sons, and the chiefs of the demigods are Your attendants, still, Your pastimes [here in Vraja] are most wonderful.

questions.question: Despite having the entire universe as His residence and Lakṣmī as His consort, why are the pastimes of Lord Kṛṣṇa considered unique?
options: Because they take place in the humble setting of Vraja
Because they involve the direct participation of Lord Brahmā
Because they represent the highest form of opulence in the universe
Because they involve the secret guidance of the demigod chiefs
questions.correct_answer: Because they take place in the humble setting of Vraja
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly contrasts Kṛṣṇa's grand position—owning the universe, having Lakṣmī as a consort, and having demigods as attendants—with the wonderful nature of His pastimes specifically in Vraja.

questions.question: Which specific figures are identified in the verse as being the attendants of Lord Kṛṣṇa?
options: The residents of Vraja and the gopīs
The chiefs of the demigods
The sages and the Vedic scholars
The kings of the earthly realms
questions.explanation: The verse states that the chiefs of the demigods are His attendants.

103.verse_text: All glories to Kṛṣṇa, the most beneficent in all the three worlds, whose forehead is marked with musk-tilaka and whose love and joy are increased by the love-sports of Vraja's milkmaids!

questions.question: What specific mark adorns the forehead of the most beneficent Lord Kṛṣṇa?
options: A mark of sandalwood paste
A mark of musk-tilaka
A mark of gopī-candana
A mark of saffron paste
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that Lord Kṛṣṇa's forehead is marked with musk-tilaka.

questions.question: Which group's love-sports contribute to the increased joy and love of the most beneficent Lord Kṛṣṇa?
options: The residents of Dvārakā
The sages of the forest
The milkmaids of Vraja
The demigods of the heavenly planets
questions.explanation: The verse specifies that Kṛṣṇa's love and joy are increased by the love-sports of Vraja's milkmaids.

104.verse_text: O Lord, You and You alone are my worshipful Deity, giver of love, fulfiller of desire. You alone are my knowledge, my power and wealth, my vital force, and my very life.

questions.question: Which specific attributes does the devotee ascribe to the Lord as the source of his vital force and very existence?
options: Knowledge, power, and wealth
Beauty, fame, and strength
Mercy, compassion, and truth
Dharma, artha, and kāma
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly identifies the Lord as the devotee's knowledge, power, and wealth, as well as his vital force and very life.

questions.question: How does the devotee characterize the Lord's role in relation to his personal desires and love?
options: As the protector of his family and honor
As the giver of love and the fulfiller of desire
As the destroyer of ignorance and fear
As the guide on the path of liberation
questions.correct_answer: As the giver of love and the fulfiller of desire
questions.explanation: The verse addresses the Lord directly as the giver of love and the fulfiller of desire.

105.verse_text: O Lord, let our words be empowered to sweetly describe Your beauty, and let our thoughts swell with eagerness when dwelling on Your childhood.

questions.question: What two specific activities does the devotee pray for in relation to the Lord's form and history?
options: Empowered speech for His beauty and eager thoughts on His childhood
Divine songs for His pastimes and deep focus on His flute-playing
Eloquent prayers for His mercy and constant meditation on His lotus feet
Sweet descriptions of His pastimes and intense longing for His presence
questions.correct_answer: Empowered speech for His beauty and eager thoughts on His childhood
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly requests that words be empowered to sweetly describe the Lord's beauty and that thoughts swell with eagerness when dwelling on His childhood.

106.verse_text: Let there ever flow in my heart the nectarous pastimes tasted by the tongues of blessed, fortunate souls; Your mischievous childhood pranks (such as laying in wait to obstruct Rādhā); and the sportive gestures of Your lotus face, inspired by Your rippling flute-song.

questions.question: Which specific mischievous act performed by Lord Kṛṣṇa is cited as a subject for constant remembrance in the heart?
options: Stealing the garments of the gopīs
Breaking the pots of butter in the homes of the residents of Vraja
Laying in wait to obstruct Rādhā
Taming the serpent Kāliya in the Yamunā river
questions.correct_answer: Laying in wait to obstruct Rādhā
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly identifies the act of laying in wait to obstruct Rādhā as one of the mischievous childhood pranks that should flow in the devotee's heart.

questions.question: What primary element serves as the inspiration for the sportive gestures of Kṛṣṇa's lotus face?
options: The sweet fragrance of the forest flowers
His own rippling flute-song
The adoring gazes of the gopīs
The beauty of the full moon in Vṛndāvana
questions.explanation: The verse mentions that the sportive gestures of His lotus face are inspired by His rippling flute-song.

107.verse_text: O Lord, if I am engaged in firm devotional service to You, then I can very easily perceive Your divine youthful form. And as far as liberation is concerned, she stands at my door with folded hands, waiting to serve me, and all material conveniences of religiosity, economic development, and sense gratification stand with her.

questions.question: What is the prerequisite for the practitioner to easily perceive the divine youthful form of the Lord?
options: Rigorous performance of severe austerities
Firm engagement in devotional service
Deep meditation on the scriptures
Complete abandonment of the material world
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that if one is engaged in firm devotional service, then one can very easily perceive the Lord's divine youthful form.

questions.question: How does the author describe the demeanor of liberation when she approaches the devotee?
options: She bows down in silent reverence
She remains distant, waiting to be summoned
She stands at the door with folded hands
She offers all material wealth as a gift
questions.correct_answer: She stands at the door with folded hands
questions.explanation: The verse states that liberation stands at the devotee's door with folded hands, waiting to serve.

questions.question: Which material conveniences are described as waiting alongside liberation to serve the devotee?
options: Religiosity, economic development, and sense gratification
Fame, wealth, and power
Knowledge, renunciation, and mystic perfection
Health, longevity, and peace of mind
questions.correct_answer: Religiosity, economic development, and sense gratification
questions.explanation: The text mentions that all material conveniences of religiosity, economic development, and sense gratification stand with liberation, waiting to serve.

108.verse_text: All glories, all glories, all glories to You, O Lord, whose holy name is auspicious for all three worlds! All glories, all glories, all glories to You, Lord Kṛṣṇa! You are the incarnation who are nectar for the ear, mind, and eyes.

questions.question: Which specific faculties are described as being nourished by the nectar of Lord Kṛṣṇa's incarnation?
options: The heart, the soul, and the breath
The ear, the mind, and the eyes
The voice, the intellect, and the spirit
The senses, the life force, and the limbs
questions.correct_answer: The ear, the mind, and the eyes
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly identifies Lord Kṛṣṇa as the incarnation who acts as nectar for the ear, mind, and eyes.

109.verse_text: I bow down to You, Kṛṣṇa, who shine forth in the ecstatic states of those very pious souls adorned with repeated agitations occasioned by a spontaneous deluge of excessive transcendental delight. I bow to You, who are the ornament of glorious Gokula and who are the unique ocean of sweet, loving tenderness glowing in the distance beyond words and thought. To You, to whatever glory You embody, I bow down.

questions.question: What specifically characterizes the internal condition of those pious souls who behold the radiant form of Lord Kṛṣṇa?
options: A profound, silent meditation on the void
Repeated agitations caused by a spontaneous deluge of excessive transcendental delight
A fearful trembling caused by the magnitude of His cosmic form
A scholarly analysis of the Vedic scriptures
questions.correct_answer: Repeated agitations caused by a spontaneous deluge of excessive transcendental delight
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that Kṛṣṇa shines forth in the ecstatic states of pious souls who are adorned with repeated agitations occasioned by a spontaneous deluge of excessive transcendental delight.

questions.question: How is Lord Kṛṣṇa described in relation to the realm of Gokula?
options: As the king who rules over the forests of Gokula
As the child who plays in the dust of Gokula
As the ornament of glorious Gokula
As the protector of the cows in Gokula
questions.correct_answer: As the ornament of glorious Gokula
questions.explanation: The verse describes Lord Kṛṣṇa as the ornament of glorious Gokula.

questions.question: Beyond words and thought, what is the nature of the ocean that Lord Kṛṣṇa represents?
options: An ocean of cosmic intelligence
An ocean of sweet, loving tenderness
An ocean of absolute destruction
An ocean of material desires
questions.correct_answer: An ocean of sweet, loving tenderness
questions.explanation: The verse identifies Kṛṣṇa as the unique ocean of sweet, loving tenderness glowing in the distance beyond words and thought.

110.verse_text: With the production of this bouquet of verses, which has the lasting fame of Dāmodara as its capital and which adorns īśānadeva's lotus feet, Līlāśuka has written about You, O Lord Kṛṣṇa, this nectar for Your ears. May it continue to flow (to be heard, chanted, and sung) for one hundred days of Brahmā.

questions.question: What entity does the author identify as the capital of the bouquet of verses composed in Śrī Kṛṣṇa-karṇāmṛtam?
options: The infinite mercy of Rādhārāṇī
The lasting fame of Dāmodara
The eternal pastimes of Gokula
The transcendental beauty of the flute
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the bouquet of verses has the lasting fame of Dāmodara as its capital.

questions.question: To whose lotus feet is the bouquet of verses composed by Līlāśuka offered as an adornment?
options: Lord Brahmā
Śrī Caitanya Mahāprabhu
īśānadeva
The milkmaids of Vraja
questions.explanation: The text specifies that the bouquet of verses adorns īśānadeva's lotus feet.

questions.question: What is the desired duration for the continued flow of the nectar composed by Līlāśuka?
options: For one hundred years of human life
For the duration of the current yuga
For one hundred days of Brahmā
Throughout all eternity without end
questions.correct_answer: For one hundred days of Brahmā
questions.explanation: The verse expresses the wish that the nectar it contains may continue to flow for one hundred days of Brahmā.

111.verse_text: Repeatedly pouring an indescribable shower of nectar into the ear-holes of those fortunate souls who relish, through repetition, the fragrance of its waves of poetic sweetness, this nectar fo the ears of Kṛṣṇa, who is sunk deep in the minds and eyes of the beautiful-eyed milkmaids (and in ours), surges in voices and ears.

questions.question: Where does the nectar describing Kṛṣṇa's sweetness remain deeply submerged according to the prayer?
options: In the minds and eyes of the beautiful-eyed milkmaids
In the hearts of the great sages and forest dwellers
In the vibrations of the flute and the peacock feather
In the sacred river Yamunā and the groves of Vṛndāvana
questions.correct_answer: In the minds and eyes of the beautiful-eyed milkmaids
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that this nectar of the ears of Kṛṣṇa is sunk deep in the minds and eyes of the beautiful-eyed milkmaids.

questions.question: To what is the experience of the poetic sweetness of the nectar compared in the text?
options: The fragrance of waves
The light of the moon
The depth of the ocean
The melody of the flute
questions.explanation: The verse mentions those fortunate souls who relish, through repetition, the fragrance of its waves of poetic sweetness.

112.verse_text: O Kṛṣṇa, always remembering the nectarous, low, soft sounds of Your flute, as well as Your eyes, doubly expanded with mercy, I pray that wherever my sight shall wander, You will always manifest Your beauty, sweetness, and opulence before me.

questions.question: What specific physical characteristics of Lord Kṛṣṇa does the devotee recall to seek His manifestation everywhere?
options: His lotus feet and peacock feather
The low, soft sounds of His flute and His eyes expanded with mercy
His yellow garments and His golden necklace
His smiling face and the fragrance of His body
questions.correct_answer: The low, soft sounds of His flute and His eyes expanded with mercy
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly identifies the nectarous, low, soft sounds of the flute and eyes doubly expanded with mercy as the features being remembered.

questions.question: What three qualities does the devotee pray to see manifested before them wherever their sight wanders?
options: His youth, intelligence, and strength
His beauty, sweetness, and opulence
His pastimes, compassion, and divine form
His music, fragrance, and transcendental abode
questions.explanation: The devotee prays for Lord Kṛṣṇa to manifest His beauty, sweetness, and opulence before them wherever they look.
